about.title: About this Project
about.methodology_text: We crawl DBLP via SPARQL and OpenReview for first author names of papers published at ~416 CCF-rated CS conferences (2010-2026), then classify names into 17 language groups using an ensemble of fastText language detection and rule-based surname matching.
about.disclaimer_text: Names are terrible predictors of language. We did it anyway. This is meant as a fun, light-hearted visualization — not a rigorous sociolinguistic study. No discrimination intended.
about.src_dblp: Author names, publication data, and venue information
about.src_openreview: Supplementary paper data and acceptance rates for AI/ML conferences (NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, etc.) — often available months before DBLP
about.src_ccf_pdf: Conference list, CCF rankings and categories (extracted from official PDFs)
about.src_fasttext: fastText lid.176.bin model for name-to-language classification
about.src_or_affiliation: First-author institution data from author profiles (current-year conferences)
about.src_martenlienen: Pre-compiled affiliation dataset for NeurIPS, ICML, and ICLR (2006-2024)
about.src_papercopilot: Pre-compiled affiliation data for ICLR, CoRL, and other conferences (2023+)
about.src_openalex: First-author institution data via title matching. Coverage varies by publisher.
about.affiliation_accuracy_text: Affiliation data is sourced from multiple providers with varying coverage. Historical data (NeurIPS/ICML/ICLR) uses pre-compiled datasets with 85-99% coverage. Current-year data uses OpenReview profiles. Other conferences use OpenAlex, where coverage depends on publisher: IEEE/ACM/Springer conferences typically have 70-90% coverage. The coverage percentage shown on each conference page indicates data completeness.
about.accuracy_text: Name-based language inference is inherently imprecise. Many factors (immigration, marriage, cultural mixing) make names unreliable indicators. Treat all results as rough approximations.
about.data_cleaning_intro: The raw DBLP data undergoes extensive cleaning before analysis:
about.clean_workshop: Workshop filtering — Keyword and abbreviation pattern detection (e.g., ICCVW, DSN-W) removes co-located workshop papers
about.clean_proceedings: Proceedings volume filtering — Removes editor entries, frontmatter, "Revised Selected Papers", and other non-paper records
about.clean_crossyear: Cross-year deduplication — Resolves publication-year vs. conference-year mismatches in DBLP using URI-based year extraction
about.clean_joint: Joint conference deduplication — Detects and removes double-counted papers from joint events (e.g., EMNLP/CoNLL, ACL/IJCNLP)
about.clean_colocation: Co-location cleanup — Separates main conference papers from co-located events sharing a single DBLP entry
about.clean_gapfill: Gap filling — Recovers missing data via DBLP Search API, OpenReview API, HTML scraping, journal supplements, and conference website APIs
about.open_data_text: The cleaned dataset (900k+ first-author records across 413 conferences, 2010–2026) is freely available for download on
